summary: Dataset passed all critical validation checks. 20,000 rows and 6 columns present. No missing values, no fully-duplicate rows. Two minor warnings: class imbalance (3.2:1 ratio) and 2 duplicate clean_reviewText entries. Feature count is below the 10-feature threshold, but this is expected given the dataset's text-centric nature. Outliers in exclamation_count are plausible real-world values.

feature_count_gte_10_excl_target.note: Only 5 features excluding target. Dataset is text-centric; primary signal lives in clean_reviewText. Acceptable for NLP-based training.

duplicate_clean_reviewText.note: 2 rows share identical clean_reviewText values. Likely genuine repeated reviews; low impact.

exclamation_count.note: Max value is 87. High exclamation counts are plausible in real reviews; no corrective action required.

class_balance.note: Moderate imbalance (3.2:1). Trainer should consider class weighting or oversampling strategies.

feature_consistency.note: review_length >= word_count for all rows (character count >= word count, as expected).

issues: WARN: Only 5 features excluding target (threshold: 10). Dataset is text-centric; primary signal is in clean_reviewText.
WARN: 2 duplicate clean_reviewText entries detected (not full-row duplicates).
WARN: exclamation_count has 1,478 outliers (7.39%) by IQR method — max value is 87. Likely real-world noise, not data errors.
WARN: Class imbalance — Positive:Negative ratio is 3.2:1 (15,233 vs 4,767). Trainer should apply class weighting or resampling.

llm_decision.reason: No critical issues found. Zero missing values, zero fully-duplicate rows, consistent engineered features, valid binary target, and two confirmed text columns. All warnings are minor and expected for a text-based sentiment dataset.

notes.data_preprocessor: 2 duplicate clean_reviewText entries exist. Consider deduplicating on clean_reviewText if exact-duplicate reviews are undesirable for training.
notes.trainer: Class imbalance (3.2:1) detected. Consider using class_weight='balanced' or oversampling the minority class (Positive=0) before training.
